label: Dynatrace
description: Connect to Dynatrace for full-stack observability and APM data including metrics, traces, logs, and infrastructure health.

form.title: Connect Dynatrace
form.subtitle: Paste your Dynatrace API token and environment URL to start pulling observability data.

methods.label: API Token
methods.description: Paste a Dynatrace API token with the required scopes alongside your environment URL. The simplest and most common path for all Dynatrace deployment types.
methods.how_to:
## Generate a Dynatrace API Token

1. Open your Dynatrace environment (e.g. `https://abc12345.live.dynatrace.com`).
2. Navigate to **Settings → Access tokens** (or use the shortcut: click your account icon → **Access tokens**).
3. Click **Generate new token**.
4. Give it a descriptive name like `Anton Integration`.
5. Under **Select scopes from the list below**, enable the scopes you need. Common ones include:
   - `metrics.read` — read metric data
   - `logs.read` — read log data
   - `entities.read` — read monitored entities
   - `problems.read` — read problem feed
   - `DataExport` — export raw data
   - `ReadConfig` — read configuration
6. Click **Generate token**. Copy the token immediately — it is shown **only once**.
7. Paste the token into the **API Token** field below.
8. Enter your environment URL (e.g. `https://abc12345.live.dynatrace.com`) into the **Environment URL** field.

## Notes

- For Managed deployments, your URL is typically `https://your-domain/e/YOUR_ENV_ID`.
- For Grail/Platform environments, the base URL format may differ — check your browser's address bar while in the Dynatrace UI.
- If the token is lost, delete it and generate a new one; Anton can be updated with the new token at any time.
- Follow the principle of least privilege — only enable scopes Anton actually needs.

fields.name: environment_url
fields.label: Environment URL
fields.description: Your Dynatrace environment base URL. For SaaS it looks like https://<env-id>.live.dynatrace.com; for Managed use https://<host>/e/<env-id>.

fields.name: api_token
fields.label: API Token
fields.description: Generated under Settings → Access tokens. Must include the scopes required for the data you want Anton to access (e.g. metrics.read, logs.read, entities.read).

methods.label: OAuth 2.0 Client Credentials
methods.description: Use a Dynatrace OAuth 2.0 client (client ID + secret) for platform API access. Required for newer Grail-based APIs and Dynatrace Platform environments.
methods.how_to:
## Create a Dynatrace OAuth 2.0 Client

This method is required when using Grail-based APIs (e.g. the Dynatrace Platform or DQL queries) and is not needed for classic SaaS environments.

1. Open your Dynatrace account at [myaccount.dynatrace.com](https://myaccount.dynatrace.com).
2. Go to **Identity & access management → OAuth clients**.
3. Click **Create client**.
4. Enter a name (e.g. `Anton`) and optionally a description.
5. Under **Scopes**, add the scopes you need:
   - `storage:metrics:read`
   - `storage:logs:read`
   - `storage:entities:read`
   - `storage:events:read`
6. Set the **Redirect URI** to `http://127.0.0.1` (Anton uses a local redirect for the code exchange).
7. Click **Save**. Copy the **Client ID** and **Client secret** — the secret is shown only once.
8. Also note your **Account URN** (shown on the client details page) as you may need it for certain API calls.
9. Paste the Client ID and Client secret into the fields below, then click **Authorize**.

## Notes

- OAuth clients live at the Dynatrace account level, not per environment — ensure the correct environment is scoped if your account spans multiple.
- If the client secret is lost, regenerate it from the OAuth clients page and update Anton.

fields.name: environment_url
fields.label: Environment URL
fields.description: Your Dynatrace Platform environment URL. For Grail/Platform this typically ends in .apps.dynatrace.com.

fields.name: client_id
fields.label: OAuth Client ID
fields.description: Found under Identity & access management → OAuth clients in myaccount.dynatrace.com.

fields.name: client_secret
fields.label: OAuth Client Secret
fields.description: Shown once when the OAuth client is created. Regenerate from the client details page if lost.
